title: Sign Up Page (`page.tsx`)
content:
## Sign Up Page (`page.tsx`) This component handles the user sign-up process for the DocMed application. It integrates with Clerk for authentication and provides a visually engaging sign-up flow with animated UI elements. ### Key Features - **Email and Password Sign-up:** Users can create an account using their email address and a password. - **Email Verification:** After initial sign-up, users are prompted to enter a verification code sent to their email. - **Google OAuth Integration:** Provides an option for users to sign up using their Google account. - **Animated UI:** Leverages custom UI components like `AnimatedForm`, `Ripple`, `TechOrbitDisplay`, and `BoxReveal` to create a dynamic and modern user experience. - **Responsive Design:** Adapts to different screen sizes, with the orbit display hidden on smaller screens. ### Component Breakdown - **`OrbitIcon` Component:** A helper component to render icons within the orbiting display, applying specific styling and ring effects. - **`orbitIcons` Array:** Configuration for the `TechOrbitDisplay` component, defining the icons, their animations (duration, delay, radius), and appearance. ### State Management - `email`: Stores the user's email address. - `password`: Stores the user's password. - `code`: Stores the email verification code. - `pendingVerification`: A boolean flag to control the visibility of the verification code input form. - `error`: Stores any error messages during the sign-up or verification process. - `loading`: A boolean flag to indicate if an asynchronous operation (sign-up, verification) is in progress. ### Functions - **`handleSubmit`:** - Handles the initial form submission for email and password sign-up. - Calls `signUp.create` and `signUp.prepareEmailAddressVerification` from `@clerk/nextjs/legacy`. - Sets `pendingVerification` to `true` upon successful preparation. - Handles potential errors and updates the `error` state. - **`handleVerify`:** - Handles the submission of the email verification code. - Calls `signUp.attemptEmailAddressVerification`. - If verification is `complete`, it sets the active session using `setActive` and redirects to `/dashboard`. - Handles potential errors and updates the `error` state. - **`handleGoogleSignUp`:** - Initiates the Google OAuth flow using `signUp.authenticateWithRedirect`. - Configures the redirect URLs for the callback and successful completion. ### Usage Example This `page.tsx` file is intended to be used as a Next.js route (`app/sign-up/[[...sign-up]]/page.tsx`). It will render the sign-up form when the user navigates to the `/sign-up` path. ```tsx // app/sign-up/[[...sign-up]]/page.tsx // ... (import statements and component logic) export default function SignUpPage() { // ... (state and handler functions) return (
{/* Left — orbit display */} {/* ... Orbit display components */} {/* Right — form */}
{!pendingVerification ? ( <> {/* Render initial sign-up form */}

Don't have an account? Sign In

) : ( <> {/* Render email verification form */}

Check your email

Enter the code sent to {email}

setCode(e.target.value)} />
)}
); } ```
